name: poly_bevel_G2_apex_v3
description: Polygon Bevel (mesh.bevel, edit-mode Polygons) GROUP shared-corner accumulator, fully-enclosed-apex branch (internalCnt>=2 && !anyBoundary) -- task 0458 Phase 1 (+ follow-up). Valence-3 apex surrounded by 3 irregular NON-planar, non-orthogonal quads (all selected) so the apex has 3 internal edges and no boundary edge -- the fully-enclosed branch, isolated on asymmetric geometry (the naive Sigma(unit normal) accumulator and the reference's AVE_N=k*N/|N|^2 coincide ONLY on the orthogonal cube-corner case every prior grouped-poly-bevel fixture happened to sit on). group=true, inset=0.1, shift=0.1. Reference topology: 7v/3f -> 13v/9f. This fixture pins the APEX vertex's position bit-exact (orig + shift*AVE_N, no inset term -- there is no boundary edge to inset against) AND the dump's 3 ring vertices (each internalCnt==1, shared between 2 of the 3 faces, its internal edge running to this fully-enclosed apex) via the recovered `bevGenInset` mitered-corner offset (findings.md SS1/SS5, task 0458 follow-up): `orig + shift*AVE_N + inset*D/(D.U)` built from the vertex's own two group-boundary-contour edges. All 4 positions are lerp_checks single-point existence assertions, plus the overall vertex/face counts. It intentionally does NOT assert the dump's remaining (standalone, single-face) vertex positions: those touch only 1 selected face and are unaffected by this task's group-accumulator fix -- a pre-existing, separately-flagged non-group insetCorner residual (task 0458 follow-up 'bonus finding', not fixed here) remains on that path.
source: Frozen reference-editor capture via the private capture harness's polygon-bevel op (task 0458 Phase 0 dump-oracle poly_bevel_G2_apex_v3). Apex position predicted-vs-measured to 2.6e-8, ring vertices to 1.2e-8..6.4e-8 (float32 floor) using the recovered AVE_N + mitered-corner-offset laws.

provenance.harness: private capture harness (reference-editor polygon-bevel tool, headless scripted op-dump)
provenance.notes: Phase-0 dump-oracle for the group shift accumulator's fully-enclosed-apex branch. Pins the apex AND its 3 ring vertices bit-exact (task 0458 follow-up closed the ring-vertex gap via the recovered `bevGenInset` mitered-corner offset) plus the topology (vertex/face counts); the dump's remaining standalone (single-face) vertices carry a separate, pre-existing, out-of-scope residual (see bevelFacesByMask's doc comment in source/mesh.d) not asserted here.
